properties.remark: Report from mPING: Trees uprooted or snapped; Roof blown off.

A report of near zero visibility from blowing dust was received from the Chihuahuan Desert Research Institute. Wind gusts beween 40 to 50 mph were observed in the region near the time of this dust storm.

Pecos County Emergency Manager reports near zero visibility along FM 1776 between FM 1450 and the Pecos River. Multiple vehicles crashes were reported. Wind gusts up to 40 mph were observed in the region during this time.

Upton County Sherriff reports road closure and accident near Midkiff between State Highway 349 and State Highway 137. This was the result of near zero visibility due to blowing dust. Wind gusts up to 40 mph were observed in the area during this time.
Public report of less than a quarter mile visibility near Garden City along Ranch Road 33 and State Highway 158. Wind gusts of around 40 mph were observed in the region.
State Highway 349 is being shutdown between Rankin and Midkiff due to high winds and near zero visibility due to blowing dust.
Reported multiple vehicle accident with injuries along US Highway 285 between Carlsbad and Artesia. Less than a quarter mile to near zero visibility was likely occurring at the time.

Reports of multiple vehicle accidents along US Highway 285 between Carlsbad and Loving. Injuries were reported. Less than a quarter mile to near zero visibility due to blowing dust was likely occurring.
Multiple social media reports and photos of vehicle accidents due to near zero visibility due to blowing dust along US Highway 87 near the Big Spring area. Time and location are approximate.
New Mexico Highway 128 was closed west of Jal due to high winds and blowing dust resulting in near zero visibility.
Continued reports of traffic slow downs/road closures along State Highway 137 in southwest Upton County due to blowing dust and near zero visibility.
Blowing dust resulted in brownout conditions along US Highway 82 between Hope and Artesia. Traffic was stopped. Time is estimated.
Spotter sent in pictures and video of near zero visibility due to blowing dust north of Artesia.

At least 13 reported accidents by Eddy County local law enforcement, mainly on US Highway 285. Approximately 30-40 vehicles involved across all accidents. Injuries were reported, but no further information. Blowing dust along US Highway 285 between Carlsbad and Artesia reduced visibility to near zero for hours.
Phone call of quarter size hail 8 miles south of Alpine along State Highway 118. Time is estimated from radar.

Midland ASOS reported a peak wind gust of 97kts from a dry microburst associated with a weakening thunderstorm. Damage nearby supports the validity of this wind gust, but this is preliminary and not final.

A transformer block was knocked off a power pole towards the south. Location of an empty semi-trailer flipped on its side. Time is estimated from radar.
A stop sign was broken away from its base. Time is estimated from radar.
The doors to a building were blown inward and through the building. Irrigation pivots were found lying towards the southeast. Time is estimated from radar.
A fully loaded shipping container was rolled approximately 100 yards towards the southeast. Drone footage showed no clear indication of a tornado path. Time is estimated from radar.

A dusty tornado was reported west of Highway 137, northwest of Lamesa. Damage was surveyed at a home, with the awning and trees being damaged. This tornado had an estimated wind speed of 100 MPH and was rated EF-1. Open fields along the estimated tornado path limited the details of the tornado track. The tornado track is radar estimated.
Report from mPING: Hen Egg+ (2.25 in.).
Metal roof panels from a small building were found lying to the south and southeast of the building. Time is estimated from radar.
Three broken power poles were found lying to the south. These poles were broken near the base or towards the middle.
A metal irrigation spool was thrown 20 yards towards the south-southeast. Spool weighs approximately 200 lbs. Time is estimated from radar.
There were reports of damage to signs, power poles, and trees in the town of Lamesa. Time is estimated from radar.
Mesonet station 18 Lamesa 2SE.

This was the estimated path of a brief tornado in the vicinity of Sparenberg to the southeast of Lamesa. Irrigation pivots were found lying towards the southeast. This was estimated to have been due to wind speeds of approximately 90 MPH. This tornado is rated EF-1. Open dirt fields in the vicinity did not allow for further survey damage. Time is radar estimated.
There were reports of downed power lines and a downed tree along FM 846 near Knott. Time is estimated from radar.

*** 2 INJ *** A single wide manufactured home was completely destroyed by straight-line winds from a microburst. The time is estimated from radar.

Two single wide mobile homes were completely destroyed and a third was damaged due to straight-line winds from a microburst. Additionally, two power poles were broken due to a microburst. One was broken at the base and the other was broken halfway up the pole. Time is estimated from radar.
A single wide manufactured home was rolled onto its side onto two vehicles. This resulted in significant damage to the home and vehicles. The time is estimated from radar.

Damaging winds from a microburst caused significant damage to The Living Desert Zoo and Garden State Park in western Carlsbad. Several large trees were broken, fencing was damaged, and a concrete retaining wall was toppled. Time is estimated and winds are estimated to have been up to 80 MPH.

All poles were laying to the west away from the road. This was likely the result of a localized microburst with wind speeds estimated to have been up to 100 mph.
